profession_backgrounds.description: You swore a sacred oath — to a liege, a god, a cause — and broke it. Not out of weakness. Out of choice. Whatever you became after that was your own doing.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You knew exactly what you were doing when you did it. That is the part that does not leave you.

profession_backgrounds.name: Void-Touched Exile
profession_backgrounds.description: Your people drove you out when the void began to bleed through you — shadows that moved wrong, cold that came from nowhere, the look in your eyes that made animals flee. You did not choose this. You accepted it.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: They called it corruption. You call it clarity.

profession_backgrounds.name: Fallen Crusader
profession_backgrounds.description: You fought for something holy once. Light, order, justice — whichever word your order used. You saw what those words actually protected and decided the dark was more honest.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You kept the discipline. You just changed what it serves.

profession_backgrounds.name: Enforcer of the Forgotten Kingdom
profession_backgrounds.description: You served as blade and boot for a dark regime — extracting confessions, eliminating threats, maintaining fear. The kingdom is gone. The methods remain.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You have made harder men than these beg. You do not expect this to be difficult.

profession_backgrounds.name: Branded Prisoner
profession_backgrounds.description: You were taken, held, and subjected to things that no longer have names for what they did to you. You did not break. Something else happened instead. You came out the other side different — darker, harder, and no longer afraid of anything.
equipment_bonus: Nothing else — you earned everything from zero
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: They made you into this. You decided that was acceptable.

profession_backgrounds.name: Monastery Monk
profession_backgrounds.description: You were raised in a monastery — rising before dawn, sparring with a staff, memorizing scripture, and learning that discipline and faith are the same thing written in different languages. The monastery also taught Sanctification rites you know by heart.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You have walked the same stone corridor ten thousand times. You no longer need to think to be calm.

profession_backgrounds.name: Temple Healer
profession_backgrounds.description: You served in a city temple's healing wing — long shifts, too many patients, and not enough Materium. You became exceptionally skilled at Vitalism and learned to stretch every spell further than it was designed to go.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You know what a body looks like when it is about to give up. You have talked several of them out of it.

profession_backgrounds.name: Cathedral Scribe
profession_backgrounds.description: You spent years copying sacred texts and annotating darkness-banishment rites in cathedral archives. You know Dawncalling not from instinct but from a hundred annotated manuscripts. You also know more about what the church does not write down than most ordained priests.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You have read every holy text the church will let you read. You have also read several they would not.

profession_backgrounds.name: Parish Priest
profession_backgrounds.description: You ran a parish — births, deaths, arguments, marriages, fevers, grief. You did not have the luxury of specializing. You healed what needed healing, blessed what needed blessing, and kept your congregation alive through two winters that should have killed half of them.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You know what people need to hear. Sometimes you even tell them the truth.

profession_backgrounds.name: Church Inquisitor
profession_backgrounds.description: You worked the church's darker arm — investigating shadow-touched incidents, confronting corrupted clerics, and performing formal exorcisms. Dawncalling is not a school you learned. It is something you were trained in with extreme specificity.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You have seen what darkness does to people who were supposed to be safe. You do not give it the benefit of the doubt anymore.

profession_backgrounds.name: Forest Foundling
profession_backgrounds.description: You were set out as a child — an Oakspeople custom for those the forest does not claim. You survived not through tradition or teaching, but through watching, stealing, and learning when to run.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: The forest did not keep you. You took what you needed from it and kept going.

profession_backgrounds.name: Market Pickpocket
profession_backgrounds.description: You learned your trade in the crowd — small fingers, quick feet, and the ability to look completely unremarkable. You have stolen from people taller than you your entire life. Most of them never noticed.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You know exactly how people hold their coin pouches. There are about four different ways. You know all of them.

profession_backgrounds.name: Hedge Healer
profession_backgrounds.description: You picked up enough Vitalism from an old wanderer to patch wounds, pull venom, and keep a party alive when no cleric was available. You charged for it. Usually in advance.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You are not a real healer. You have kept more people alive than most real healers. You prefer not to explain the difference.

profession_backgrounds.name: Beast Tender
profession_backgrounds.description: You worked with animals — caring for them, calming them, occasionally persuading them not to kill anyone. You learned that most creatures respond to the same things: patience, smell, and knowing when to back off.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: You have been bitten by things that would make a warrior faint. You simply waited until they calmed down.

profession_backgrounds.name: Traveling Fraud
profession_backgrounds.description: You wandered town to town selling miracle cures, performing small magical tricks that looked more impressive than they were, and leaving before anyone compared notes. You got very good at the convincing part.
profession_backgrounds.flavor_trait: The trick is to leave before the second dose wears off. You have never stayed for the third.
